task_completion.description: Open the geoprocessing toolbox or menu in gvSIG
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG Geoprocessing toolbox window or a geoprocessing menu dropdown is visible on the screen.
The 'Centroids' (or 'Center of mass') tool is highlighted in the geoprocessing toolbox tree, or its specific configuration dialog is open.

task_completion.description: Set the input layer to the countries dataset
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Centroids tool dialog, the input layer dropdown or text field shows 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries'.
In the Centroids tool dialog, the output file path is set to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/country_centroids.shp'.

task_completion.description: Execute the tool and generate the point layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new point layer (e.g., 'country_centroids') appears in the Table of Contents (TOC) on the left, and/or point features are visible on the map view, located at the center of the country polygons.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG graphical user interface to perform the geoprocessing task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with gvSIG dialogs and menus, rather than terminal windows running command-line GIS tools (like ogr2ogr or python scripts).

integrity.description: The output layer was genuinely created through the tool, not spoofed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tool execution dialog, progress bar, or the resulting layer being rendered in the gvSIG map view is visible, showing actual GIS software behavior.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to fake the output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) are seen open and editing shapefile components or metadata to bypass the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input layer is 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries'. The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/country_centroids.shp'. A correct execution will result in a point shapefile containing exactly 177 features (one for each country polygon in the Natural Earth 1:110m dataset).
